title: Fork Attribution Dispute
description: Developer A claims Developer B forked their open-source library, removed attribution, rebranded it as proprietary software, and is now selling commercial licenses. Developer B argues the MIT license allows this and their version has substantial original work that differentiates it from the original.
evidence: Original repo with MIT license, 2,400 stars, created 18 months ago
Forked repo: 89% code similarity per git diff analysis
Developer B's commercial license terms charging $500/year
Git commit history showing Developer B's first commit matches Developer A's codebase
Developer B claims 11% original code constitutes 'substantial transformation'
